title: Salary Calculator — Hourly to Annual Salary Converter
description: Convert hourly wage to annual salary or annual to hourly. Calculate weekly, biweekly, monthly, and yearly earnings instantly.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: How do I convert an hourly wage to an annual salary?
faq.a1: Multiply your hourly rate by the number of hours you work per week, then multiply by the number of weeks you work per year. For a standard 40-hour week and 52-week year: Annual = Hourly × 40 × 52. For example, $25/hour × 40 × 52 = $52,000 per year.
faq.q2: How is the daily rate calculated?
faq.a2: The daily rate is computed by dividing the annual salary by 261 — the approximate number of working days in a year (52 weeks × 5 days). For a $52,000 salary: $52,000 / 261 ≈ $199.23 per day. This is a standard used by HR and payroll systems in the US.
faq.q3: What is the difference between biweekly and semimonthly pay?
faq.a3: Biweekly pay is paid every two weeks — 26 pay periods per year. Semimonthly pay is paid twice a month — 24 pay periods per year. This calculator uses the biweekly (26 period) standard. The difference matters for budgeting: biweekly results in two extra paycheck months each year.
faq.q4: Does this calculator account for taxes or deductions?
faq.a4: No. This calculator shows gross salary figures only, before any federal, state, or local taxes, Social Security, Medicare, health insurance, or retirement contributions. For net take-home pay after federal taxes, use the Tax Calculator.
faq.q5: What if I work part-time or seasonal hours?
faq.a5: Adjust the 'Hours Per Week' and 'Weeks Per Year' fields to match your actual schedule. For example, a part-time worker at 20 hours/week for 48 weeks earning $18/hour: $18 × 20 × 48 = $17,280 annual salary.

content.whatIs: This salary calculator converts between hourly and annual wages and shows you your equivalent pay across every common pay period: daily, weekly, biweekly, monthly, and yearly.
content.formula:
If hourly: Annual = Amount × Hours/Week × Weeks/Year
If annual: Hourly = Amount / (Hours/Week × Weeks/Year)
Monthly = Annual / 12
Biweekly = Annual / 26
Weekly = Annual / 52
Daily = Annual / 261
content.example: A worker earning $25/hour at 40 hours/week for 52 weeks earns $52,000/year. That works out to $1,000/week, $2,000 biweekly, $4,333.33/month, and $199.23/day.
content.howItWorks_1: Select whether you know your hourly rate or your annual salary, then enter the amount.
content.howItWorks_2: Adjust hours per week (default 40) and weeks per year (default 52) if your schedule differs.
content.howItWorks_3: Click Calculate to see your equivalent hourly, daily, weekly, biweekly, monthly, and annual figures instantly.
